content.text:
<user_query>
Fix a UX bug in `/Users/duhl/git/ui/apps/org-next/src/routes/_authed/workspace/new.tsx`.

## Problem (P2-1: Whitespace-only input is a silent no-op)

The submit button's disabled prop was changed from `disabled={isCreating || !name.trim()}` to `disabled={isCreating}`. This means users can now submit with whitespace-only input. The handler's guard catches it:

```typescript
if (!trimmed) {
  return;
}
```

But it returns silently without calling `setFormError`, so the user gets no feedback — they click "Create workspace" and nothing happens.

## Fix

Restore the `!name.trim()` check on the disabled prop. Change line 179 from:

```typescript
<Button className="w-full" disabled={isCreating} type="submit">
```

Back to:

```typescript
<Button className="w-full" disabled={isCreating || !name.trim()} type="submit">
```

## Important context
- The file is at `/Users/duhl/git/ui/apps/org-next/src/routes/_authed/workspace/new.tsx`
- Read the file first before editing
- Only change the Button's disabled prop — do NOT touch the FormData/handleSubmit logic
- The `name` state variable is defined on line 72 as `const [name, setName] = useState("")`
</user_query>

Reading the file and restoring the submit button's `disabled` prop.

Searching the file for Button `disabled` props — the snippet already shows the fix.

Show committed version of Button lines in new.tsx

content.text:
There is nothing to change in your tree: the submit `Button` already uses `disabled={isCreating || !name.trim()}`.

```179:181:/Users/duhl/git/ui/apps/org-next/src/routes/_authed/workspace/new.tsx
            <Button className="w-full" disabled={isCreating || !name.trim()} type="submit">
              {isCreating ? "Creating workspace..." : "Create workspace"}
            </Button>
```

`git show HEAD:apps/org-next/src/routes/_authed/workspace/new.tsx` shows the same `disabled` logic on the last committed version (only JSX formatting differs from your working copy). So the whitespace-only silent no-op from `disabled={isCreating}` alone is not present in this checkout.

If you still see only `disabled={isCreating}` locally, refresh from the branch or re-merge; the intended fix is already what’s in the file. I did not edit `handleSubmit` / `FormData` per your constraints.
